content.full_text:
June 17, 1992

California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attn: Alice Hughes

Greetings:

This is a follow-up of the telephone discussion Ms. Hughes engaged in with James Pillow on June 2, 1992 at about 11:45 AM. The discussion concerned whether the League of Placer County Taxpayers was, or was not, required to file a campaign expenditure report on Form 461 or be considered an Independent Expenditure Committee.

The League of Taxpayers circulated a petition to amend the Charter of Placer County in 1991 and the petition was qualified for the June 2, 1992 ballot. In May 1992 the League had printed and circulated a flyer regarding the Charter Amendment by having the flyer inserted in portions of two newspapers in the County. Expenditures for printing and insertion totalled less than $1,000.00.

We understand from your telephone discussion with Mr. Pillow that the League is not required to file a report for an Independent Expenditure Committee with the Secretary of State or to the local (County) Election Department.

Please confirm via return letter that you concur with the above paragraph and per any pertinent details of the June 2nd discussion.

Thank you for your cooperation and assistance.

Sincerely,

Wally Reemelin
President

The image you provided is mostly blank, with very faint and mostly illegible text in the upper right corner. The majority of the content cannot be reliably transcribed or summarized due to its extremely low quality and lack of clear, readable text. If there is any specific part of the image that you believe might contain important information, please provide a higher quality scan or another attempt at scanning the page.
